met.location: MSB cupboard, ground floor plant room

assumptions: TN-C-S adopted (commercial typical with utility PEN bond, Ze=0.30Ω). The v1.2 version of this example modelled a rural TT installation; v1.3 re-anchors to the paired db-layout commercial TPN MSB scenario.

F01-F03 LV_power feeders: Zs exceeds the OCPD-only Zs_max because Type D breakers and Ze=0.30Ω constrain Zs to ≤0.046Ω. ADS is achieved via selective coordination with the downstream DB-level RCDs and OCPDs at DB-L1/DB-P1/DB-M1 — MSB feeders are not RCD-protected at MSB level (commercial practice avoiding nuisance tripping of life-safety paths).

F04 fire-alarm feeder: exempt from standard ADS per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560 (life-safety circuits). RCD intentionally omitted to maintain availability; cable monitoring + intumescent containment provide continuity assurance.
Cable resistance values for Zs are LLM-estimates using standard 70°C Cu conductor data (IEC 60228 / Eland tables). Deterministic refinement via calc.zs_loop_impedance is deferred per WI3.

flags: TOOL-CALL-PENDING:calc.zs_loop_impedance — Zs values are LLM-estimates; deterministic refinement deferred per WI3.

rationale.chat_summary: TN-C-S earthing for an INT commercial multi-tenant MSB on a utility PEN-bonded supply (Ze=0.30Ω). MET in the MSB cupboard with 70mm² main earthing conductor; 35mm² bonds to incoming water and gas, 25mm² to structural steel. Four MSB feeders (F01-F03 LV_power to lighting/power/mechanical sub-DBs, F04 fire-alarm to DB-FA1). MSB-level RCDs intentionally omitted: ADS coordinated at downstream DB level; fire-alarm feeder exempt per IEC 60364-5-56. CPCs sized per IEC 60364-5-54 §543.1.2 (S/2 rule for S>16mm²). Reply to refine, e.g. 'add a 300mA upstream RCD on F03'.

sections.title: Earthing System
sections.summary: TN-C-S adopted because the utility supplies a PEN bond at the service transformer (Ze=0.30Ω). Within the building, N and PE are separated downstream of the MSB.

decisions.label: TN-C-S system confirmed
decisions.summary: Utility declared PEN bond with Ze=0.30Ω; commercial typical.
decisions.rule: Ontology TN-C-S entry permitted for INT

sections.title: Main Earthing Terminal
sections.summary: MET in MSB cupboard, ground-floor plant room. 70mm² Cu main earthing conductor sized per IEC 60364-5-54 §542.3 (sized against the largest line conductor in the installation, F02 at 240mm² → CPC 120mm²; MET conductor sized to match the heaviest bonding requirement and the building-wide aggregate fault path).

decisions.label: MET location at MSB cupboard
decisions.summary: Co-located with the main switchboard for short bond runs and inspector access.

inputs.met_location: MSB cupboard, ground floor plant room

decisions.label: 70mm² Cu main earthing conductor
decisions.summary: Sized to handle the aggregate fault current path back to the supply earth.

sections.title: Electrodes
sections.summary: No supplementary electrode installed — utility provides the system earth via PEN bond. Optional auxiliary electrode could be added if site demands lightning protection (not declared here).

sections.title: Main Bonding
sections.summary: Three main protective bonds from MET to extraneous-conductive-parts: incoming water, incoming gas, and main column structural steel.

decisions.label: 35mm² Cu to water and gas services
decisions.summary: Sized per IEC 60364-5-54 Clause 544.1 ≥ half of largest line CSA (240mm²/2 = 120mm² cap; 35mm² adequate for service entry).

decisions.label: 25mm² Cu to structural steel
decisions.summary: Structural steel bonded at MSB cupboard; sized adequate for fault-energy dissipation.

sections.title: Supplementary Bonding
sections.summary: No supplementary bonding zones declared at the MSB itself. Each downstream sub-DB (DB-L1, DB-P1, DB-M1) will declare its own supplementary bonding requirements per zone.

sections.title: CPC + Zs Verification
sections.summary: CPCs sized per IEC 60364-5-54 §543.1.2 (adiabatic / S/2 rule) for the LV_power feeders (S>16mm²) and per Table 54.2 for the fire-alarm feeder (S=16mm²). Zs computed inline using 70°C Cu cable resistance data.

decisions.label: CPC sizing rule S/2 for S>16mm² (F01-F03)
decisions.summary: Adiabatic-equivalent simplification — IEC 60364-5-54 §543.1.2 permits CPC = S/2 for S>16mm² in copper.

decisions.label: Zs exceeds OCPD-only Zs_max on F01-F03
decisions.summary: With Ze=0.30Ω and Type D MCCBs (Ia=20·In), Zs_max is ≤0.046Ω which the feeder cable alone cannot meet. ADS achieved via selective coordination with downstream DB-level RCDs at DB-L1/DB-P1/DB-M1.
decisions.rule: IEC 60364-4-41 §411.4.2 — alternative ADS via RCD when overcurrent path insufficient

sections.title: RCD Requirements
sections.summary: MSB-level RCDs intentionally omitted on F01-F03 to avoid nuisance tripping of life-safety paths; ADS is provided by selective downstream DB-level RCDs. F04 fire-alarm feeder is exempt from standard ADS per IEC 60364-5-56 (life-safety availability priority).

decisions.label: No RCD at MSB level on LV_power feeders
decisions.summary: Selective coordination strategy — RCDs at downstream DBs handle final-circuit ADS without disrupting upstream selectivity.

decisions.label: Fire-alarm feeder exempt from ADS
decisions.summary: IEC 60364-5-56 (life-safety installations) prioritises availability; fire-alarm circuits are exempted from automatic disconnection that would compromise the safety service.

sections.title: Compliance
sections.summary: Compliant. ADS strategy documented as selective downstream coordination + fire-alarm exemption. One TOOL-CALL-PENDING flag for deterministic Zs refinement.

sections.title: Assumptions
sections.summary: Four assumptions captured: TN-C-S re-anchor (replacing rural TT scenario), selective-coordination ADS strategy for F01-F03, fire-alarm ADS exemption for F04, and LLM-estimated cable resistance values pending deterministic refinement.
